questions.question: How does the article describe Notion?

options.text: A dynamic notebook to manage class work, projects, and other college tasks.
options.explanation: The article opens by calling Notion a “dynamic notebook” for school‑related work.

options.text: A static spreadsheet for tracking grades.
options.explanation: Notion is not described as a spreadsheet; it’s a flexible, dynamic workspace.

options.text: A video‑editing platform for creating lecture recordings.
options.explanation: Video editing is unrelated to Notion’s core purpose in the guide.

options.text: A graphic‑design tool for creating presentation slides.
options.explanation: Design tools are not mentioned; Notion focuses on note‑taking and collaboration.

questions.question: Who can upgrade to the Plus plan according to the guide?

options.text: Anyone with a student or educator email address.
options.explanation: The guide explicitly states that a student or educator email qualifies for a Plus upgrade.

options.text: Only users with a corporate email address.
options.explanation: Corporate emails are not mentioned as a criterion for the Plus plan.

options.text: All free‑tier users regardless of email domain.
options.explanation: The upgrade is limited to specific academic email domains, not everyone.

options.text: Only users who have previously purchased a Notion add‑on.
options.explanation: No prior purchase is required; the email address determines eligibility.

questions.question: Which feature is NOT included in the Plus plan?

options.text: Unlimited guest sharing
options.explanation: Unlimited guest sharing is listed as a Plus benefit.

options.text: Ability to upload large files
options.explanation: Uploading large files is explicitly mentioned as part of the Plus plan.

options.text: Access to earlier versions of pages
options.explanation: Earlier version history is a Plus feature.

options.text: Real‑time video conferencing
options.explanation: The guide never mentions video conferencing; it’s not part of the Plus plan.

questions.question: Where can you find additional templates beyond those shown in the sidebar’s template picker?

options.text: The Notion Template Gallery
options.explanation: The article directs users to the Template Gallery for more options.

options.text: The Settings page
options.explanation: Settings are not discussed as a source of extra templates.

options.text: The Help Center
options.explanation: The Help Center provides assistance, not a repository of templates.

options.text: The official Notion YouTube channel
options.explanation: YouTube is mentioned for video tutorials, not for browsing templates.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as a template type available in the sidebar’s picker?

options.text: Class notes
options.explanation: Class notes are explicitly mentioned as a template option.

options.text: Reading lists
options.explanation: Reading lists are listed among the starter templates.

options.text: Thesis planning
options.explanation: Thesis planning is one of the suggested templates.

options.text: Financial accounting
options.explanation: Financial accounting is not mentioned in the guide’s template examples.

questions.question: Approximately how many templates can you browse in the Notion Template Gallery?

options.text: Over 10,000
options.explanation: The guide states you can browse “over 10,000 templates” in the gallery.
The number 500 is far lower than the figure given in the article.
While 1,000 is a plausible number, the guide specifies a larger count.
The guide mentions a number greater than 5,000.

questions.question: Which statement best explains the advantage of unlimited guest sharing in the Plus plan for a collaborative academic project?

options.text: It lets any number of collaborators view or edit pages without storage limits, and earlier page versions let the team track changes—features the free plan lacks.
options.explanation: Unlimited guest sharing removes caps on collaborators, and together with version history and unlimited storage, it supports large, evolving projects—benefits not available on the free tier.

options.text: It only allows guests to view pages, not edit them, which is the same as the free plan.
options.explanation: The Plus plan provides editing rights for guests, not just view‑only access.

options.text: It provides extra storage but does not include version history, so changes cannot be tracked.
options.explanation: Version history is explicitly listed as a Plus benefit, so this statement is inaccurate.

options.text: It is intended for personal use only and cannot be used for group collaboration.
options.explanation: Unlimited guest sharing is designed for collaboration, especially in academic settings.
